zotero.abstractNote: Normalizing flows are a powerful class of generative models that learn a transformation from a simple base distribution to a complex target distribution. However, they are often limited by the choice of the base distribution and the expressivity of the transformation. In this work, we propose a novel method to improve the performance of normalizing flows by bootstrapping the base distribution. We show that our method can be applied to a variety of flow-based models and leads to improved performance on several density estimation tasks.
zotero.conferenceName: 1st ELLIS Machine Learning for Molecule Discovery Workshop
